comments: The PRD contains three critical architectural flaws and blast-radius risks. 1. BLAST RADIUS (git clean -fdx): The PRD mandates running 'git clean -fdx' after checking out the master branch. This is catastrophic. The workspace's .gitignore includes crucial directories like 'node_modules/', 'dist/', '__pycache__/', and '*.log'. Running '-x' will obliterate all local developer environments, required dependencies, and the dozens of audit/debug logs currently in the workspace. The next SDLC run will fail instantly because its dependencies will be missing. 2. INCONSISTENCY: The Requirements section demands 'git clean -fdx', but the Architecture section specifies 'git clean -fd'. 3. PYTHON EXCEPTION HIERARCHY BUG: The PRD instructs to 'Wrap the main execution loop in a try...except Exception block' to catch SIGINT/SIGTERM. In Python, KeyboardInterrupt (triggered by SIGINT) inherits from BaseException, not Exception. An 'except Exception:' block will fail to catch the interrupt, causing the orchestrator to exit without running the 'os.killpg' reaper logic, completely defeating the Process Group Isolation design and leaving orphaned processes behind.
